title: Rolled beeswax taper candles
subtitle: Honeycomb-sheet tapers, no melting required
excerpt: Beeswax taper candles rolled from pre-formed honeycomb sheets. No melting, no moulds, no thermometer. The sheets roll tightly around a cotton wick at room temperature; a standard 20 × 20 cm sheet makes one 20 cm taper in under 5 minutes.
sourceNotes: Mountain Rose Herbs beeswax sheet candle guide (mountainroseherbs.com, accessed May 2026), rolling technique and wick sizing. Bramble Berry rolled taper notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026).

content.text: Rolled beeswax taper candles from honeycomb sheets. Pre-formed beeswax sheets are pliable at room temperature (18°C or above); they roll tightly around a cotton wick without any melting. Each sheet is approximately 20 × 20 cm; this recipe uses two sheets to make a matching pair of standard 20 cm tapers.

If the sheets feel stiff and resist rolling without cracking, warm them for 15-20 seconds on a radiator or with a brief pass of a hair dryer on a low setting. The sheets should bend without breaking before you start rolling.

items.name: Beeswax pellets
items.prepNote: pre-formed beeswax honeycomb sheets, approximately 20 × 20 cm each. Available from candle-making suppliers in natural yellow or bleached white. One sheet per candle.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: square-braid cotton wick, cut to 22 cm (2 cm longer than the sheet width). No pre-tabbing needed; the wick extends slightly beyond the base of the candle.

content.text: Place the wick and begin the first roll

Lay the beeswax sheet flat on a clean surface with a long edge towards you. Press the wick along the bottom long edge, leaving approximately 1 cm extending beyond the sheet at one end; this will be the wick at the top of the candle after rolling. The wick should sit in the first groove of the honeycomb texture.
Fold the bottom edge of the sheet tightly over the wick, pressing firmly to seal the wax against the wick. The seal needs to be snug; a loose first fold leads to a gap and the candle burns unevenly.
Roll the sheet tightly and evenly

Continue rolling the sheet around the wick, pressing down firmly along the entire length of the sheet with each roll so the layers adhere. Keep the bottom edge of the sheet level; any diagonal drift makes the candle lean. When you reach the far edge, press the sheet edge firmly against the candle surface to seal; the warmth of your hands is enough to fuse the final edge.

Press the base of the candle firmly flat on the work surface so it will stand without a candleholder. Trim the base wick flush with the base. Trim the top wick to 5 mm. Repeat for the second candle. Roll both candles to match the same diameter for a matching pair.

Rolled beeswax tapers burn in a narrow pool around the wick. The outer beeswax wall remains solid; this is the characteristic taper burn. Burn in a draught-free location; tapers are much more susceptible to flame movement than container candles. Never leave a burning taper unattended or near flammable materials. Burn on a suitable candleholder or in a holder that catches drips.

Effectively indefinite. Store in a cool, dry location away from direct heat or sunlight, which causes the sheets to soften and the candle to deform. Store upright or wrapped individually in tissue paper; the honeycomb texture can imprint onto the surface of adjacent candles if stacked under pressure.

recipe.yieldDescription: 2 matching taper candles (~15-20 burn hours each)
